variations.name: Main Line

annotation.moveRationale: Bxd2+ exchanges material, altering the pawn structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Nc6 develops toward the center, supporting the pawn structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Qe7 activates the queen, increasing pressure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Be7 develops the bishop to a modest but solid square, preparing to castle in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
c5 challenges the center from the flank in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Bb7 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Bg2 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
b6 prepares the fianchetto, aiming to place the bishop on a long diagonal in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
g3 prepares the fianchetto, aiming to place the bishop on a long diagonal in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
a5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Bd2 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Nbd2 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Nfd2 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
b5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
d5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.

Nc3 develops toward the center, supporting the pawn structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.

Nf3 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
e6 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
c4 gains space on the queenside in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
Nf6 develops a piece to an active square in the Bogo-Indian Defense.
d4 claims the center and opens the diagonal for the queen's bishop, characteristic of the Bogo-Indian Defense.
